paper.title: Internalizing Tool Knowledge in Small Language Models via QLoRA Fine-Tuning

sources.label: arXiv HTML paper
sources.notes: Paper identity, training setup, prompt reduction, and retention evidence.

sources.label: GitHub search for official repo

sources.notes: No candidate repo returned in this local pass.

reference_repo_influences: paper2code: mark implementation details paper-only when no repo exists
MinerU: parser option for paper source
MinerU-Document-Explorer: future tool-use paper corpus index
algorithm_traits: tool knowledge examples plus question-to-plan examples plus execution traces
prompt-token reduction is paired with retention risk
QLoRA setup is not a drop-in replacement for MAformac MLX LoRA

maformac_insertion_points.recommendation: Future C5 data categories for tool_knowledge, question_to_plan, execution_trace, and retention probes.
Future experiment comparing full D-domain catalog, retrieved catalog, and internalized/no-catalog prompt.
Future C6 branch for description-free inference with tool selection, argument, dependency, state/readback, and retention gates.
Backend compatibility spike required before any QLoRA or quantized-adapter claim.

gate.rationale: The paper is directly relevant to internalizing D-domain tool knowledge, but no official repo was found and the reported Qwen3 retention loss requires guarded future evaluation.
stop_conditions: No C6 rebuild without future OpenSpec authorization.

residual_risks: No official code branch to verify implementation.
Benchmark domain differs from vehicle cockpit control.
Qwen3 retention degradation may be unacceptable for MAformac.
A100/QLoRA setup does not map directly to MLX-on-Mac constraints.
